title: Second-order moment equivalence of twisted Gaussian Schell model beams and orbital angular momentum eigenmodes
authors: T. Ferreira, G. Santos, S. Ayala, Lucas Hutter, E. S. Gómez, G. Lima, G. Cañas, S. P. Walborn
abstract: We show that the covariance matrix of any cylindrically symmetric coherent orbital angular momentum (OAM) eigenmode with quantum number $\ell$ takes a universal form depending only on $\langle r^2\rangle$, $\langle k_r^2\rangle$, and $\ell$, independently of the radial profile, and that this form is identical to the covariance matrix of a twisted Gaussian Schell-model (TGSM) beam.} More specifically, both matrices share the same pattern of zero and nonzero entries, with the off-diagonal blocks proportional to $\ell$ and the TGSM twist parameter $u$, respectively. This result holds for an arbitrary radial profile and provides direct term-by-term identification of parameters between the two sets of beams. We work out the correspondence in detail for three important families: Laguerre--Gaussian (LG), Bessel--Gaussian, and perfect vortex beams (PVBs), and derive the conditions under which each coherent OAM mode maps onto a physically realizable TGSM beam. {Because the covariance matrix governs second-moment evolution under arbitrary ABCD (symplectic) transformations, any two beams sharing the same covariance matrix are second-order indistinguishable at every propagation plane. In particular, the matched TGSM and coherent OAM beams share identical beam-width evolution, far-field divergence, and $M^2$ beam-quality factor.} In particular, the well-developed TGSM propagation toolbox applies directly to the second-order moment evolution of the three coherent families. We further show that within each beam family the covariance matrix uniquely determines the beam parameters, with exact uniqueness established for LG modes. Additional results include cross-family second-moment equivalence conditions and a proof that PVB modes form a complete orthonormal basis in the limit $w\to 0$.
summary: This paper proves that the second-order statistical description (covariance matrix) of any cylindrically symmetric OAM beam is mathematically identical to that of a Twisted Gaussian Schell-model (TGSM) beam. This equivalence allows researchers to use the well-established TGSM propagation toolbox to predict the beam-width evolution and divergence of complex OAM modes. It is significant because it unifies the description of coherent and partially coherent light in terms of their second-order moments.
main_problem: The paper establishes a rigorous mathematical equivalence between the second-order moment (covariance matrix) of coherent Orbital Angular Momentum (OAM) eigenmodes and Twisted Gaussian Schell-model (TGSM) beams.
main_result: The covariance matrix of any cylindrically symmetric OAM eigenmode takes a universal form identical to that of a TGSM beam, allowing for direct, second-order indistinguishable parameter mapping between the two beam types.
method: The analysis involves deriving and comparing the covariance matrices for both beam types, utilizing the fact that the covariance matrix governs second-moment evolution under symplectic transformations.
model_or_system: The study compares two models: (1) Coherent OAM eigenmodes (including Laguerre-Gaussian, Bessel-Gaussian, and Perfect Vortex Beams) and (2) Partially coherent TGSM beams, both analyzed under the assumption of cylindrical symmetry.
key_observables: Covariance matrix elements ($\langle r^2 angle, \langle k_r^2 angle$), beam-width evolution, far-field divergence, and the $M^2$ beam-quality factor.
important_parameters: OAM quantum number ($\ell$), TGSM twist parameter ($u$), beam waist ($w$), and coherence parameters ($\sigma, \delta$).
main_assumptions: The equivalence is established for the second-order moments, and certain analyses for PVB and BG beams rely on leading-order approximations in specific parameter regimes.
figures_summary: The notes reference a summary table (Table I) detailing the correspondence between the covariance matrix parameters for TGSM, LG, PVB, and BG beams.
paper_structure: The paper develops the theory for both beam types independently, then compares their covariance matrices to derive the parameter mapping. It details the correspondence for specific families (LG, BG, PVB) and concludes by showing the implications for beam propagation and basis completeness.
why_interesting: This work provides a powerful, non-trivial connection between the description of highly structured, coherent light (OAM modes) and the description of partially coherent light (Schell model), unifying their second-order propagation characteristics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
